+{"id":"bd-bqcc","title":"Consolidate maintenance commands into bd doctor --fix","description":"Per rsnodgrass in GH#692:
> "The biggest improvement to beads from an ergonomics perspective would be to prune down commands. We have a lot of 'maintenance' commands that probably should just be folded into 'bd doctor --fix' automatically."

Current maintenance commands that could be consolidated:
- clean - Clean up temporary git merge artifacts
- cleanup - Delete closed issues and prune expired tombstones
- compact - Compact old closed issues
- detect-pollution - Detect and clean test issues
- migrate-* (5 commands) - Various migration utilities
- repair-deps - Fix orphaned dependency references
- validate - Database health checks

Proposal:
1. Make `bd doctor` the single entry point for health checks
2. Add `bd doctor --fix` to auto-fix common issues
3. Deprecate (but keep working) individual commands
4. Add `bd doctor --all` for comprehensive maintenance

This would reduce cognitive load for users - they just need to remember 'bd doctor'.

Note: This is higher impact but also higher risk - needs careful design to avoid breaking existing workflows.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-22T14:27:31.466556-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T14:27:31.466556-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-bs5j","title":"Release v0.33.2","description":"Version bump workflow for beads release 0.33.2.

## Variables
- `0.33.2` - The new version number (e.g., 0.31.0)
- `2025-12-21` - Release date (YYYY-MM-DD format)

## Workflow Steps
1. Kill running daemons
2. Run tests and linting
3. Bump version in all files (10 files total)
4. Update cmd/bd/info.go with release notes
5. Commit and push version bump
6. Create and push git tag
7. Update Homebrew formula
8. Upgrade local Homebrew installation
9. Verify installation

## Files Updated by bump-version.sh
- cmd/bd/version.go
- .claude-plugin/plugin.json
- .claude-plugin/marketplace.json
- integrations/beads-mcp/pyproject.toml
- integrations/beads-mcp/src/beads_mcp/__init__.py
- README.md
- npm-package/package.json
- cmd/bd/templates/hooks/* (4 files)
- CHANGELOG.md

## Manual Step Required
- cmd/bd/info.go - Add versionChanges entry with release notes","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:10:13.759062-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T17:29:31.791368-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-21T17:29:31.791368-08:00","deleted_by":"batch delete","delete_reason":"batch delete","original_type":"epic","wisp":true}
 {"id":"bd-bw6","title":"Fix G104 errors unhandled in internal/storage/sqlite/queries.go:1181","description":"Linting issue: G104: Errors unhandled (gosec) at internal/storage/sqlite/queries.go:1181:4. Error: rows.Close()","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-07T15:35:09.008444133-07:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T23:13:40.536627-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T16:46:11.029355-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-bwk2","title":"Centralize error handling patterns in storage layer","description":"80+ instances of inconsistent error handling across sqlite.go with mix of %w, %v, and no wrapping.

Location: internal/storage/sqlite/sqlite.go (throughout)

Problem:
- Some use fmt.Errorf("op failed: %w", err) - correct wrapping
- Some use fmt.Errorf("op failed: %v", err) - loses error chain
- Some return err directly - no context
- Hard to debug production issues
- Can't distinguish error types

Solution: Create internal/storage/sqlite/errors.go:
- Define sentinel errors (ErrNotFound, ErrInvalidID, etc.)
- Create wrapDBError(op string, err error) helper
- Convert sql.ErrNoRows to ErrNotFound
- Always wrap with operation context

Impact: Lost error context; inconsistent messages; hard to debug

Effort: 5-7 hours","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-11-16T14:51:54.974909-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T21:44:37.237175-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T21:44:37.237175-08:00","close_reason":"Already implemented: errors.go exists with sentinel errors (ErrNotFound, ErrInvalidID, ErrConflict, ErrCycle), wrapDBError/wrapDBErrorf helpers that convert sql.ErrNoRows to ErrNotFound, and IsNotFound/IsConflict/IsCycle checkers. 41 uses of wrapDBError, 347 uses of proper %w wrapping, 0 uses of %v. Added one minor fix to CheckpointWAL."}

+{"id":"bd-u2sc","title":"GH#692: Code quality and refactoring improvements","description":"Epic for implementing refactoring suggestions from GitHub issue #692 (rsnodgrass). These are code quality improvements that don't change functionality but improve maintainability, type safety, and performance.

Original issue: https://github.com/steveyegge/beads/issues/692

High priority items:
1. Replace map[string]interface{} with typed structs for JSON output
2. Adopt slices.SortFunc instead of sort.Slice (Go 1.21+)
3. Split large files (sync.go, init.go, show.go)
4. Introduce slog for structured logging in daemon

Lower priority:
5. Further CLI helper extraction
6. Preallocate slices in hot paths
7. Polish items (error wrapping, table-driven parsing)","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-22T14:26:31.630004-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T14:43:08.562025-08:00","external_ref":"gh-692"}
+{"id":"bd-u2sc.1","title":"Replace map[string]interface{} with typed JSON response structs","description":"Many CLI commands use map[string]interface{} for JSON output which loses type safety and compile-time error detection.

Files with map[string]interface{}:
- cmd/bd/compact.go (10+ instances)
- cmd/bd/cleanup.go
- cmd/bd/daemons.go
- cmd/bd/daemon_lifecycle.go

Example fix:
```go
// Before
result := map[string]interface{}{
    "status": "ok",
    "count": 42,
}

// After
type CompactResponse struct {
    Status string `json:"status"`
    Count  int    `json:"count"`
}
result := CompactResponse{Status: "ok", Count: 42}
```

Benefits:
- Compile-time type checking
- IDE autocompletion
- Easier refactoring
- Self-documenting API","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T14:26:44.088548-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T14:26:44.088548-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-u2sc.1","depends_on_id":"bd-u2sc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-22T14:26:44.088931-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
+{"id":"bd-u2sc.2","title":"Migrate sort.Slice to slices.SortFunc","description":"Go 1.21+ provides slices.SortFunc which is cleaner and slightly faster than sort.Slice.

Found 15+ instances of sort.Slice in:
- cmd/bd/autoflush.go
- cmd/bd/count.go
- cmd/bd/daemon_sync.go
- cmd/bd/doctor.go
- cmd/bd/export.go
- cmd/bd/import.go
- cmd/bd/integrity.go
- cmd/bd/jira.go
- cmd/bd/list.go
- cmd/bd/migrate_hash_ids.go
- cmd/bd/rename_prefix.go
- cmd/bd/show.go

Example migration:
```go
// Before
sort.Slice(issues, func(i, j int) bool {
    return issues[i].Priority < issues[j].Priority
})

// After
slices.SortFunc(issues, func(a, b *types.Issue) int {
    return cmp.Compare(a.Priority, b.Priority)
})
```

Benefits:
- Cleaner 3-way comparison
- Slightly better performance
- Modern idiomatic Go","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T14:26:55.573524-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T14:26:55.573524-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-u2sc.2","depends_on_id":"bd-u2sc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-22T14:26:55.573978-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
+{"id":"bd-u2sc.3","title":"Split large cmd/bd files into logical modules","description":"Several cmd/bd files exceed 1000 lines and should be split for maintainability:

| File | Lines | Recommendation |
|------|-------|----------------|
| sync.go | 2201 | Split: sync_core.go, sync_branch.go, sync_remote.go |
| init.go | 1742 | Split: init.go, init_wizard.go, init_hooks.go |
| show.go | 1380 | Split: show.go, show_formatters.go, show_threads.go |
| compact.go | 1112 | Split: compact.go, compact_tiers.go |

Guidelines:
- Each file should focus on one aspect
- Keep public API in main file
- Move helpers/formatters to separate files
- Consider internal/ packages for complex logic

Note: This is a lower priority task - large files are inconvenient but not blocking.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T14:27:06.146343-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T14:27:06.146343-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-u2sc.3","depends_on_id":"bd-u2sc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-22T14:27:06.146704-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
+{"id":"bd-u2sc.4","title":"Introduce slog for structured daemon logging","description":"The daemon currently uses fmt.Fprintf for logging which produces unstructured output. Migrating to slog would provide:

- Structured, parseable logs (JSON format option)
- Log levels (debug, info, warn, error)
- Context propagation
- Better log aggregation

Files to update:
- cmd/bd/daemon_logger.go
- Debug output throughout daemon code

Example:
```go
// Before
fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to detect user role: %v\n", err)

// After
slog.Warn("failed to detect user role", "error", err)
```

This is particularly valuable for daemon troubleshooting since daemon logs are often reviewed after the fact.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T14:27:16.47144-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T14:27:16.47144-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-u2sc.4","depends_on_id":"bd-u2sc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-22T14:27:16.471878-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-umbf","title":"Design contributor namespace isolation for beads pollution prevention","description":"## Problem

When contributors work on beads-the-project using beads-the-tool, their personal work-tracking issues leak into PRs. The .beads/issues.jsonl is intentionally tracked (it's the project's issue database), but contributors' local issues pollute the diff.

This is a recursion problem unique to self-hosting projects.

## Possible Solutions to Explore

1. **Contributor namespaces** - Each contributor gets a private prefix (e.g., `bd-steve-xxxx`) that's gitignored or filtered
2. **Separate database** - Contributors use BEADS_DIR pointing elsewhere for personal tracking
3. **Issue ownership/visibility flags** - Mark issues as "local-only" vs "project"
4. **Prefix-based filtering** - Configure which prefixes are committed vs ignored

## Design Considerations

- Should be zero-friction for contributors (no manual setup)
- Must not break existing workflows
- Needs to work with sync/collaboration features
- Consider: what if a "personal" issue graduates to "project" issue?

## Expansion Needed

This is a placeholder. Needs detailed design exploration before implementation.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-13T18:00:29.638743-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-13T18:00:41.345673-08:00"}

The 'bond' verb captures:

2. Dependency linking (child issues bonded in a DAG)
3. Short and active

 {"id":"bd-xsl9","title":"Remove legacy autoflush code paths","description":"## Problem

The autoflush system has dual code paths - an old timer-based approach and a new FlushManager. Both are actively used based on whether flushManager is nil.

## Locations

- main.go:78-81: isDirty, needsFullExport, flushTimer marked 'used by legacy code'
- autoflush.go:291-369: Functions with 'Legacy path for backward compatibility with tests'

## Current Behavior

```go
// In markDirtyAndScheduleFlush():
if flushManager != nil {
    flushManager.MarkDirty(false)
    return
}
// Legacy path for backward compatibility with tests
```

## Proposed Fix

1. Ensure flushManager is always initialized (even in tests)
2. Remove the legacy timer-based code paths
3. Remove isDirty, needsFullExport, flushTimer globals
4. Update tests to use FlushManager

## Risk

Low - the FlushManager is the production path. Legacy code only runs when flushManager is nil (test scenarios).","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T15:49:30.83769-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T15:49:30.83769-08:00"}

+{"id":"bd-y0fj","title":"Issue lifecycle hooks (on-close, on-complete)","description":"Add hooks that fire on issue state transitions, enabling automation like closing linked GitHub issues.

## Problem

We have `external_ref` to link beads issues to external systems (GitHub, Linear, Jira), but no mechanism to trigger actions when issues close. Currently:

```
bd-u2sc (external_ref: gh-692) closes → nothing happens
```

## Proposed Solution

### Phase 1: Shell Hooks

Add `.beads-hooks/on-close.sh` (and similar lifecycle hooks):

```bash
# .beads-hooks/on-close.sh
# Called by bd close with issue JSON on stdin
#\!/bin/bash
issue=$(cat)
external_ref=$(echo "$issue" | jq -r '.external_ref // empty')
if [[ "$external_ref" == gh-* ]]; then
    number="${external_ref#gh-}"
    gh issue close "$number" --repo steveyegge/beads \
      --comment "Completed via beads epic $(echo $issue | jq -r .id)"
fi
```

### Lifecycle Events

| Event | Trigger | Use Cases |
|-------|---------|-----------|
| `on-close` | Issue closed | Close external refs, notify, archive |
| `on-complete` | Epic children all done | Roll-up completion, close parent refs |
| `on-status-change` | Any status transition | Sync to external systems |

### Phase 2: Molecule Completion Handlers

Molecules could define completion actions:

```yaml
name: github-issue-tracker
on_complete:
  - action: shell
    command: gh issue close {{external_ref}} --repo {{repo}}
  - action: mail
    to: mayor/
    subject: "Epic {{id}} completed"
```

### Phase 3: Gas Town Integration

For full Gas Town deployments:
- Witness observes closures via beads events
- Routes to integration agents via mail
- Agents handle external system interactions

## Implementation Notes

- Hooks should be async (don't block bd close)
- Pass full issue JSON to hook via stdin
- Support hook timeout and failure handling
- Consider `--no-hooks` flag for bulk operations

## Related

- `external_ref` field already exists (GH#142)
- Cross-project deps: bd-h807, bd-d9mu
- Git hooks: .beads-hooks/ pattern established

## Use Cases

1. **GitHub integration**: Close GH issues when beads epic completes
2. **Linear sync**: Update Linear status when beads status changes  
3. **Notifications**: Send mail/Slack when high-priority issues close
4. **Audit**: Log all closures to external system","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-22T14:46:04.846657-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T14:50:40.35447-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T14:50:40.35447-08:00","close_reason":"Molecules already cover this use case. Completion actions should be encoded as tail steps in molecules rather than lifecycle hooks. This keeps everything in the beads data plane, makes it resumable/auditable, and allows tiered delegation (haiku for simple steps). Hooks would escape the ledger and add a parallel system without clear benefit."}
